{{Quote|Good morning Princess, have you got anything to declare?|Sergeant Rolex|<ref name="marvel48" />}}
'''Rolex''' was the [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant-at-Arms]] for the [[Bank of Aargau/Legends|Bank of Aargau]] [[Bank of Aargau Security, Ltd.|Security]] [[Planetary Customs Security Checkpoint LH48]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A neat and punctual<ref name="aargau2">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030814aargau2|series=Aargau: For All Your Banking Needs|text=Part 2: Secure Lines of Credit}}</ref> [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]],<ref name="marvel48" /> Rolex's duties included conducting security checks for visitors to [[Aargau/Legends|Aargau]] and keeping a daily log, in which he recorded information on customs checks and an hourly incident log detailing consequences dealt to violators of Aargau's customs rules.<ref name="aargau3">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030821aargau3|series=Aargau: For All Your Banking Needs|text=Part 3: Now 99% Crime Free}}</ref>

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Rolex performed the customs checks for [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] when they visited Aargau on business.<ref name="marvel48" /> Afterward, Rolex served as a Customs Field Investigator looking into a series of disturbances surrounding Organa and Vader's corresponding visits. Although Rolex pursued a connection between the two warring leaders' arrival and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Li-Suun Niik]], this avenue proved fruitless, and he later recommended that Organa and Vader both be immediately arrested on sight if they returned to Aargau.<ref name="aargau4">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030828aargau4|series=Aargau: For All Your Banking Needs|text=Part 4: Checks, Balances, and Overdrafts}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Rolex first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 48|''Star Wars'' (1977) 48]], written by [[Larry Hama]] and released in [[1981]]. In the comic, Rolex was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]].<ref name="marvel48" /> The [[2003]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] article ''[[Aargau: For All Your Banking Needs]]'' later contained several passages written from Rolex's point of view, and containing additional information about his role.<ref name="aargau2" /><ref name="aargau4" />
